rows.source_ref: thomaas, 'Price Action made simple with Supply and Demand levels', ForexFactory thread 452780, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/452780-price-action-made-simple-with-supply-and-demand (author's free companion site www.21pips.com; method credited to Sam Seiden S&D videos and other FF threads)
rows.concept: A consolidation 'base' preceding a strong impulse move is an unfilled supply/demand zone; a fresh first retest of that zone reverses price, entered with a limit order for a fixed risk:reward.
rows.eligibility_reason: Core mechanic (impulse+base zone detection, fresh-only limit retest, SL beyond distal edge, fixed 1:2-1:3 TP) is deterministic and pure-MQL5 buildable, but the 'filter bad levels' and 'precisely draw levels' sections are [To come] and never delivered, so departure-strength / base-size thresholds must be parametrized by us.
rows.notes: Zone types DBD/DBR/RBR/RBD; author says RBD and DBR are best. Filters: freshness (first touch only), strong departure (2-3 big same-colour candles), few candles in base. Entry at proximal edge, SL few pips beyond distal edge, TP fixed 1:2 or 1:3, risk 2.5%, pending orders, ~1-2 trades/wk. Primarily EUR/USD H1 but author claims any pair/TF and specifically notes gold (XAUUSD) works well -> testable on FX majors/crosses + XAUUSD. LOW priority: this is a classic support/resistance / S&D chart-pattern mean-reversion, the exact family the FX-edge doctrine (07-16) and Wyckoff/SMC closure (07-19/20) treat as structurally dead; brings no uncorrelated return driver. Variant in-thread (FerruFx #47): wait for a reversal pattern + momentum-break confirmation at the zone instead of a blind limit -> same core, fold into this row. redteamgo's discretionary crude/NG order-flow scaling is not mechanizable and not a separate candidate.
